type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py

type: QUESTION
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: What is the create_jinja_env(filters=...) signature/location you're adding? I need to merge global registry filters into env.filters there to avoid clobbering your edit
blocked_on: agent1 create_jinja_env implementation

type: ANSWER
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: create_jinja_env(loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Environment, defined right after the prompt() decorator (after get_fn_name etc are declared below it, but Python resolves at call time so order is fine). It builds env with loader+trim_blocks+lstrip_blocks+keep_trailing_newline+StrictUndefined, sets built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None. Used by Prompt._template_from_str/_template_from_file which now both take an optional filters param. If you merge a global registry, do it inside create_jinja_env by updating env.filters with the registry dict BEFORE applying the per-call filters param, so per-call filters still win on name conflicts.
